title: Golden Globes Nominations Snubs and Surprises | The Oscars Show
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins woke up at 5 a.m. for the 77th Golden Globe nominations and go through them category by category: the De Niro snub, Netflix crowding out Ford v Ferrari, Cats left for dead. Then they read the tea leaves on the Oscar race and talk with documentarian Alex Gibney about Citizen K.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Front-runner status, honored up and down the slate, but De Niro was snubbed for Best Actor, read as payback for mocking the HFPA on a telecast years ago.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Six nominations, but no Best Director for Baumbach. Hit Netflix this weekend and drove the conversation.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Five nominations. Kyle Buchanan floated it as a possible A Star Is Born of the season; Brad and Leo both got in.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Four nominations including Todd Phillips for director, running eighth on the oddsboards. Sean's Green-Book-style pick to actually win Best Drama.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Three nominations including screenplay; ineligible for drama or comedy under the HFPA's foreign-language rule. The screenplay nod signals it's being taken seriously.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Three nominations. Daniel Craig got in on star power once Sandler moved out of the comedy field.

films.title: The Two Popes
films.note: Golden Globes candy: Vatican setting, Pryce and Hopkins, feel-good and accessible. Premieres on Netflix Dec 20.
The serious-dad movie of the season; nudged into Best Drama over Ford v Ferrari.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Only two nominations and no Best Drama, a big loser of the morning. One of four woman-led films the Globes overlooked.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Two nominations, no Lulu Wang for director or screenplay. Ran in drama, not comedy, complicated by its Chinese production.

films.title: Bombshell
films.note: Two nominations, missed Best Drama. Margot Robbie in supporting; Charlize Theron's transformation called unreal on rewatch.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: One nomination, for J.Lo: expected, but nothing else.

films.title: Cats
films.note: One nomination. 'Dead, at least in the awards race.' Sean keeps insisting it was snubbed.

films.title: Richard Jewell
films.note: One nomination, for Kathy Bates in supporting. Too Eastwood-American for the HFPA, but rising in critics lists.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: Zero nominations, three weeks before release, may toll the end of its campaign.

films.title: Waves
films.note: Zero nominations; ends the conversation completely.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Christian Bale in Best Actor but no Best Picture. A traditional crowd-pleaser squeezed out by Netflix's four-film haul.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: No love for Pattinson or Dafoe. The film the top-five audience begged Sean to cover. 'Keep shining.'

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: Moved from comedy into drama after last week's taping, neutralizing Sandler's chances. Shut out.

films.title: Dolemite Is My Name
films.note: Two nominations, a campaigning win for Netflix. Watch whether Eddie Murphy makes the Best Actor race.

films.title: Pain and Glory
films.note: Best Foreign Language nominee; Antonio Banderas in Best Actor.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.note: A nice surprise in Foreign Language: strong opening box office, one of the best-reviewed films of the year, and not France's Oscar submission.

films.title: Les Misérables
films.note: Ladj Ly's film, in Best Foreign Language.

films.title: Atlantics
films.note: Mati Diop's Netflix film, the big Foreign Language snub.

films.title: Invisible Life
films.note: Brazil's Oscar entry; Sean caught it over the weekend and recommends it.

films.title: Frozen II
films.note: Best Animated nominee in a weak year for the category; 'Into the Unknown' also up for song (Idina Menzel, not Panic! at the Disco).

films.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World
films.note: Best Animated nominee.

films.title: Missing Link
films.note: Laika/UA production with an aggressive swag-heavy campaign; looks exposed once the Oscar race narrows.

films.title: Toy Story 4
films.note: Best Animated nominee, one of three Disney nods in the category.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: Nominated in Animated at the Globes; won't compete there at the Oscars.

films.title: I Lost My Body
films.note: The critics' animated pick, snubbed here: a strange, very good French Netflix film whose oddity may have limited the HFPA.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: In Best Comedy; 'I'm Gonna Love Me Again' up for song (Elton John).

films.title: Harriet
films.note: Cynthia Erivo in Best Actress/Drama; 'Stand Up' up for song. A movie Sean and Amanda find fine but uninteresting.

films.title: Wild Rose
films.note: Jessie Buckley is delightful and the song is lovely. Shame on the HFPA for missing it.

films.title: Motherless Brooklyn
films.note: A random-but-welcome Best Score nomination (Daniel Pemberton) capturing mid-century New York jazz.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Shut out of screenplay and score (Giacchino); starting to feel like it's fading.

films.title: Us
films.note: Michael Abels' score snubbed; Lupita Nyong'o's Best Actress snub is a blow despite Universal's billboard push.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: No score nomination, no Star Wars at all. Hadn't screened in time.

films.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
films.note: Tom Hanks in supporting; missed Best Drama.

films.title: The Report
films.note: Annette Bening in supporting, Sean's 'nothing burger' pick: 'she's just wearing a wig.'

films.title: Judy
films.note: Renée Zellweger a lock in Best Actress/Drama; category fraud that it isn't in comedy. Possibly this year's Glenn Close, though she already has an Oscar.

films.title: Clemency
films.note: Alfre Woodard snub in Best Actress, last-minute, few have seen it.

films.title: Where'd You Go, Bernadette
films.note: Cate Blanchett in Best Comedy Actress, a movie Amanda thinks doesn't work but isn't quite bad.

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: Beanie Feldstein in Best Comedy Actress, a cheerful Globes-stage moment if she wins.

films.title: Late Night
films.note: Emma Thompson in Best Comedy Actress.

films.title: Honey Boy
films.note: Noah Jupe in the young-actor race, running behind Julia Butters.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Amanda's beloved Critics' Choice Best Sci-Fi nominee; Sean keeps calling the Thanos snub 'brutal.'

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Stock up: New York and NBR both named it best film. Getting Roma vibes: critics love it, but the older, watch-my-friends crowd has been resistant. Reached ~13.2M viewers in five days per Nielsen.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: The best-picture weather vane: as soon as film Twitter started asking 'are we sure it's that good,' Amanda called it. Manageable, quietly artistic, and 'anyone who's been cheated on is like, fuck this movie.'

films.title: Roma
films.note: The comparison point: swept the critics last year, then a lot of people just meant to get through it at home.

films.title: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie
films.note: Debuted to ~11M views but didn't crack the Oscar conversation. Popularity doesn't equal awards.

films.title: Bird Box
films.note: The meme-fueled benchmark: ~26M views in five days, zero Oscars.

films.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
films.note: Stock down: cooked except for Tom Hanks, whom Sean really likes. Doing okay business, on year-end lists.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Stock down: didn't even make the AFI top 10, which is where a movie like it wants to be.

films.title: Citizen K
films.note: His latest: the story of Mikhail Khodorkovsky, once Russia's richest man, jailed after calling out Putin's corruption and now a dissident in exile. A pocket history of Russia's false democracy told as pulp: gangster film, Western, dark comedy. Title nods to both Kane and Kafka's K.

films.title: Taxi to the Dark Side
films.note: His Oscar winner. Approached as a torture film; found the way in through Dilawar, the taxi driver, and told it as a murder mystery. The first film he narrated himself, after including footage of his dying father.

films.title: Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief
films.note: Cited among the films that made his name.

films.title: The Inventor: Out for Blood in Silicon Valley
films.note: His other 2019 release, also dark, also about what ambition does to a person.

films.title: Gonzo: The Life and Work of Dr. Hunter S. Thompson
films.note: Rights trouble stalled it, so he worked on it alongside Taxi to the Dark Side, the accidental start of his habit of juggling projects.

films.title: Enron: The Smartest Guys in the Room
films.note: His first big film, narrated by Peter Coyote; he approached it as a heist film.

films.title: Casino Jack and the United States of Money
films.note: The Jack Abramoff film. Left him still hunting a lobbyist willing to name names about money in politics.

films.title: Client 9: The Rise and Fall of Eliot Spitzer
films.note: One of the films where he used actors to play with fiction and reality.

films.title: Zero Days
films.note: Another where he experimented with actors and reenactment.

films.title: Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese
films.note: Got the playful fiction/non-fiction conceit but found the real Dylan footage (the Baez exchanges, the performances) far more interesting than the invented mythology.

films.title: For Sama
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he's seen, raw and tender, a first-time filmmaker documenting horror in Syria while raising a child. 'Out of despair comes hope.'
